# Nevada Flu Report Code repository

1. Generates Nevada’s weekly influenza‐surveillance figures & tables  
   (`flu_report` pipeline).  
2. Uploads ESSENCE hospital data to ILINet and downloads QA extracts  
   (`essence_to_ilinet` pipeline).
3. Redcap upload

Everything (Python **and** R) ships **inside** the repo, so you do *not* need

* Administrator rights  
* Anything on your system `PATH`  
* A separate R or Python install  

---

## Folder map

```{text}
nevada-flu-report/
│
├─ run_flu_report.bat          # One-click Windows launcher (calls the venv)
│
├─ .venv/                      # Python virtual-environment  ← git-ignored
│   └─ …                       #
│
├─ tools/                      # Third-party runtimes you ship with the repo
│   └─ R-4.5.2/                # Portable R + required CRAN packages
│
├─ src/                        # Editable source code (PEP 517/518 layout)
│   ├─ flu_report/             # Weekly report pipeline          (existing)
│   │   └─ …                   #
│   │
│   └─ essence_to_ilinet/      # NEW: ESSENCE → ILINet uploader
│       ├─ __init__.py
│       ├─ main.py             # `python -m essence_to_ilinet …`
│       ├─ pipeline.py         # End-to-end workflow
│       ├─ facility_map.py     # Hospital-ID lookup table
│       └─ requirements.txt    # Extra pins (mirrors root)
│
├─ outputs/                    # ILINet template TXT files (auto-generated)
├─ downloads/                  # QA CSVs & other artefacts (auto-generated)
└─ README.md
```


## One-time workstation setup (no admin rights)

### Get the code

```{text}
Download the ZIP release and unzip from 
https://github.com/your-org/nevada-flu-report.git
```

### Set credentials as user environment variables
**You only need to do this once per machine / Windows account**

```{powershell}
cd C:\where\you\unzipped\nevada-flu-report
notepad .env          # or use VS Code / any editor
```

**Add the following lines to the `.env` file, replacing the placeholders with your actual credentials.**
```{text}
# .env  – DO NOT commit this file to GitHub
ESSENCE_USER=my_essence_username
ESSENCE_PASS=my_essence_password

ILINET_USER=my_ilinet_username
ILINET_PASS=my_ilinet_password

REDCAP_TOKEN=my_redcap_token
REDCAP_URL=https://redcap.yourdomain.org/api/
```

### Make Rscript discoverable
```{powershell}
#(adjust the path if your R version or install folder is different)

$env:Path += ';C:\Program Files\R\R-4.5.2\bin\x64'
```


### Create & activate a Python virtual-environment
```{powershell}
python -m venv .venv  
```


### Install Python dependencies + editable package
```{powershell}
python -m pip install -U pip
python -m pip install -e .
python -m pip install -r src/essence_to_ilinet/requirements.txt
python -m pip install playwright
python -m playwright install chromium
```

## Run the report generator

### flu_report pipeline

All season-specific parameters live in src/flu_report/config.py.

**Edit SEASON_START and SEASON_END (first Sunday → last Saturday) each September.**

```{powershell}
$Env:ILINET_DOWNLOAD_DIR = "$PWD\downloads"
python -m flu_report.main --year 2026
```

### essence_to_ilinet pipeline
1. Downloads ESSENCE CSV for the date range & facilities
2. Builds ILINet TXT template in outputs/ESSENCE_ILINet_YYYY-MM-DD.txt
3. Logs into ILINet, uploads the TXT, downloads ILI & Lab QA CSVs
4. Saves QA files to downloads/ with today’s date in the filename
```{powershell}
python -m essence_to_ilinet `
       --start 10Aug2025 `
       --end   28Feb2026
```
